content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 7, 1989


Vincent F. Biondo, Jr.
City Attorney
City of Carlsbad
1200 Elm Street
Carlsbad, CA  92008-1989

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-022

Dear Mr. Biondo:

     This is in response to your request for advice regarding
application of the mass mailing provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

     Does the roster listing printed on the arts office fact
sheet, which includes the members of the city council and
arts commission, comply with the mass mailing restriction of
Proposition 73?

CONCLUSION

     The roster listing printed on the arts office fact
sheet, which includes the members of the city council and
arts commission, complies with the mass mailing provisions of
Proposition 73.

FACTS

     As the city attorney for the city of Carlsbad, you want
to ensure that your city's arts office fact sheet is in
compliance with the new mass mailing provisions amended into
the Act by Proposition 73.  Proposition 73 is the campaign

________________________________________________________________________
1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Vincent F. Biondo, Jr.
February 7, 1989
Page 2

finance reform initiative adopted by the voters in the June
1988 primary election.

Attached to your letter requesting advice was a copy of
the arts office fact sheet. On one side of the fact sheet is
a description of the history of the arts office and arts commission in Carlsbad and the services provided to the public.

On the other side of the mailing is a listing of the
members of the city council and the members of the arts commission. Each officeholder's name is in the same size
typeface and type size and all are located together on the
left side of the fact sheet. Also printed with this listing
is the address of the arts office and the city seal of
Carlsbad.

ANALYSIS

A mass mailing is defined in Section 82041.5 as two
hundred or more substantially similar pieces of mail.
Government Code Section 89001 provides that no newsletter or
other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense. Read
together and taken literally, these two sections could be
construed to ban all mailings of 200 or more substantially
similar documents if the mailings are done at public expense.
(Commission Memoranda on Regulation 18901, copy enclosed)

Regulation 18901.2/ (copy enclosed), which implements
Section 89001, provides that where a mass mailing includes
the name of an elected officer in the letterhead of the
standard stationery, form and envelopes of the agency, it is
not prohibited under Section 89001, provided that the listing
of agency or committee officials or members all appear in the
same typeface, typesize and location in the mass mailing. In
addition, where the mass mailing does not use the agency's
standard stationery letterhead, a roster listing containing
the names of all elected officers in the agency may be used
in place of the agency's standard stationery letterhead.
(Regulation 18901(e).)

In applying the Commission's regulation to the mailing
you submitted with your letter, it appears that your roster
complies with the regulation. The roster contains all of
the names of the members of the city council and the arts
commission in the same typeface, size and location in the
layout.

2/ This regulation although adopted by the Commission at its
December 6, 1988 meeting is under review with the office of
Administrative Law. We anticipate that it will be in effect on
approximately April 1, 1989. We are advising that the regulation
be followed while it is undergoing that review.

Vincent F. Biondo, Jr.
February 7, 1989
Page 3

If I can be of any further assistance to you, please
feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JWW:aa

Enclosure

CITY OF CARLSBAD
1200 ELM AVENUE
CARLSBAD, CALIFORNIA 92008-1989
(619) 434-2891

VINCENT F. BIONDO, JR.
CITY ATTORNEY

RONALD R. BALL
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

January 6, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

RE: MASS MAILINGS

My city management is experiencing significant difficulty in
understanding the rules for mass mailing. Any help you can give
in that regard would be very much appreciated.

In particular, this letter is to ask whether or not the enclosed
"fact sheet" can be used by our Arts Commission. Approximately
3,000 of these cards were printed last year and remain on hand.
They are not bulk mailed but are available in quantity at meetings,
are enclosed when the office applies for grants, any individual
requesting information about the Arts Commission receives one, and
they are placed in racks and are available to the public at the
library at City Hall. Our Arts Director wants to know if she can
continue to use these fact sheets with the City Council listed.
Any advice you can give in that regard would be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

VINCENT F. BIONDO, JR.
City Attorney

rmh
enclosure

c: Arts Director

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 13, 1989

Vincent F. Biondo, Jr.
City Attorney
1200 Elm Avenue
Carlsbad, CA  92008-1989

Re:  89-022

Dear Mr. Biondo:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on January 10, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329.))

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Acting General Counsel

RONALD R. BALL
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

January 6, 1989


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

RE: MASS MAILINGS

My city management is experiencing significant difficulty in
understanding the rules for mass mailing. Any help you can give
in that regard would be very much appreciated.

In particular, this letter is to ask whether or not the enclosed
"fact sheet" can be used by our Arts Commission. Approximately
3,000 of these cards were printed last year and remain on hand.
They are not bulk mailed but are available in quantity at meetings,
are enclosed when the office applies for grants, any individual
requesting information about the Arts Commission receives one, and
they are placed in racks and are available to the public at the
library at City Hall. Our Arts Director wants to know if she can
continue to use these fact sheets with the City Council listed.
Any advice you can give in that regard would be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

VINCENT F. BIONDO, JR.
City Attorney
